title: Error unregistering shutdown handler for libpod
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

Some commands complain about a shutdown handler failing to unregister.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

```
$ pkill podman
$ pgrep pod
$ podman system reset --force
ERRO[0000] Error registering shutdown handler for libpod: handler with name libpod already exists
$ rm -r ~/.config/containers/ ~/.local/share/containers/
$ podman system reset --force
ERRO[0000] Error registering shutdown handler for libpod: handler with name libpod already exists
$ pgrep pod
$
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

No output on success.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

I am unsure of when it happens. I can reliably reproduce it using the above steps but I recall running into it while using podman-compose, though I cannot remember exactly how. I'll see if I can get a repro.

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      3.0.0-dev
API Version:  3.0.0
Go Version:   go1.15.5
Git Commit:   85b412ddcdacb635e13ec67ecd2df5990dbdca02
Built:        Thu Dec  3 12:28:55 2020
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```

    name: crun

    path: /usr/bin/crun
    version: |-
      crun version 0.16

        using FUSE kernel interface version 7.31
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
podman 2.2.0+rc2+112+g85b412ddc-1
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide?**

Yes

comments.body:
i have the same error output, on any podman command as user after i updated from 2.x something to 2.2.0~2 .
i can use podman as root, but as user every command is returned with error unregistering shutdown handler for libpod.

## Package info

podman version
Version:      2.2.0
API Version:  2.1.0
Go Version:   go1.15.2
Built:        Thu Jan  1 01:00:00 1970
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```
LANG=POSIX  apt list podman podman-rootless podman-plugins | grep amd64
podman-plugins/unknown,now 1.1.1~1 amd64 [installed]
podman-rootless/unknown,now 2.2.0~2 amd64 [installed]
podman/unknown 2.2.0~2 amd64
```

That is a separate bug (`Error: cannot re-exec process`) - please open a new issue.
Meanwhile, I fixed this one with #8629 so closing.
